cot_prefix: Okay, let's look at this question. The question is asking why it was one of the author's happiest times. The passage starts with, "It was one of the happiest times of my life. I was 29 and had just received my bachelor's degree, graduating with honors despite working two jobs and being a wife and mother." So the key points here are that she just got her bachelor's degree, did it with honors, and even though she had the responsibilities of working two jobs and being a wife and mother. Looking at the answer choices, option B is "she had just received her bachelor's degree," which directly matches the beginning of the passage. Option A mentions becoming a good wife and getting two jobs, but the passage states she was already a wife and had two jobs; the happiness is tied to receiving the degree. Option C says she was a teacher at Ashland University, but the text mentions Ashland University as the place where she received her diploma, not as
